perturbation.original_text: LICENSEE may terminate this Agreement upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: LICENSEE may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation directly changes the notice period required for LICENSEE to terminate the agreement. The original text specifies 'ninety (90) days', while the modified text reduces this to 'thirty (30) days'. This creates a clear conflict regarding the proper procedure for termination, impacting the rights and obligations of both parties and creating uncertainty about the enforceability of either clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For Convenience. LICENSEE may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination notice period from ninety (90) days to thirty (30) days. The contradicted text states that the LICENSEE may terminate the agreement upon thirty (30) days' written notice. This creates an inconsistency, as the original and modified versions of the changed text now conflict with the contradicted text depending on which version of the text is used, as it specifies two different notice periods. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition: 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.
